message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "deductible applies per damage event (multi-item damage)"
Current error: Expected {payout: 600, remainingCap: 2600}, received {payout: 400, remainingCap: 2800}
Passing tests so far: 21

The new test has 2 damages in incident.damages. Each damage must be processed: find matching item by type in policy.items, compute its individual payout (with high-enchantment/dragon-material/standard rules), apply the 100G deductible per damage, then sum all payouts (clamped to cap).

Implementation hint: change claim to iterate over incident.damages, compute per-damage payout, sum them, then clamp the total to the cap.

All 21 previously passing tests must still pass. Do NOT refactor.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implementation, then make the minimal change needed.
Now I understand. I need to change `claim` to iterate over all damages, compute per-damage payout, sum them, then clamp total to cap.

content.text:
All 22 tests pass (21 previously passing + 1 newly activated).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Changed `claim` to iterate over `input.incident.damages` using `sumBy`, computing per-damage payout (which already includes the per-damage deductible via `payoutFor`), then clamping the total to the policy cap.
- Result: All tests now pass (22 passing, 2 todo).
- Approach: Minimal change — reused existing `sumBy`, `findInsuredItem`, and `payoutFor` helpers; just replaced the single-damage lookup with a sum across all damages. Single-damage tests still pass because a sum over one element equals that element.

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_04-01-47_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
